data.title: Sultan bin Ahmed visits China, tours Huawei R&D Centre and SMG.
data.description: In recent news, statistics indicate a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in the past week. Health officials reported a total of 10,000 new cases across the country, marking a 20% rise compared to the previous week. The surge has raised concerns about the effectiveness of current safety measures and vaccination efforts. The data also reveals a spike in hospitalizations, with a 15% increase in admissions. Authorities are urging the public to adhere to social distancing guidelines and get vaccinated to curb the spread of the virus. Additionally, there has been a notable rise in the percentage of positive cases, reaching 8% in the latest round of testing. As the situation evolves, experts emphasize the importance of vigilance and quick action to prevent further escalation of the pandemic.

data.title: Colombia promotes romantic travel, known for more than 5 decades, during Valentine's weekend.
data.description: Colombia is emerging as a top choice for romantic getaways and destination celebrations, going beyond its reputation for flowers and cacao. This Valentine's weekend, the country is a key part of the festivities. With over five decades of recognition, Colombia's appeal for romantic travel is on the rise.

data.title: Bitmine Immersion Technologies Inc. stock rose due to Ethereum price surge and increased institutional accumulation, particularly Charles Schwab's 59.55% increase in holdings to 2,732,761 shares. Ethereum traded at $2,029.96 with $245B market cap and $21.9B trading volume.
data.description: Bitmine Immersion Technologies Inc. saw a significant increase in its stock price on Friday, driven by the strong performance of Ethereum and increased institutional investment. Ethereum's price was at $2,029.96, reflecting a 4.24% increase in the last 24 hours and a 2.36% increase over seven days. The cryptocurrency has a market cap of $245 billion and a trading volume of $21.9 billion. Charles Schwab Corp notably boosted its holdings in Bitmine, raising its shares from 1,712,780 to 2,732,761, indicating a 59.55% increase in its position. This move aligns with the overall trend of rising institutional interest in the company.

data.title: Sandy Milton, retired COO of Piedmont Advantage Credit Union, receives Lifetime Achievement Award from Carolinas CUF.
data.description: In Winston-Salem, North Carolina on February 13, 2026, Sandy Milton, the former Chief Operating Officer of Piedmont Advantage Credit Union, graciously received recognition from colleagues in the Carolinas upon retirement. The Carolinas Credit Union Foundation honored Milton with a standing ovation, recognizing her contributions to the industry.

data.title: Big, bipartisan group progressing on crypto bill despite obstacles. SEC Chairman warns on future regulatory conflicts.
data.description: A significant bipartisan group is dedicated to advancing the Senate's crypto market structure bill, as per Sen. Mark Warner (D-VI). Despite facing challenges, talks are ongoing. SEC Chairman Paul Atkins highlighted during a Senate Banking Committee hearing that while the SEC can introduce rules under "Project Crypto," any clarity from SEC actions alone could be overturned by a future administration. Atkins emphasized the necessity of long-lasting and comprehensive crypto regulations achieved through legislation from Congress, not just agency rulemaking. The proposed Digital Asset Market Clarity Act is encountering obstacles, with disagreements still unresolved. Visit Benzinga.com for the complete article.

data.title: TransUnion (TRU) beats Q4 earnings: $1.07 per share, sales $1.171 billion vs. estimates. FY2026 EPS forecast $4.63-$4.71.
data.description: TransUnion (NYSE: TRU) reported strong fourth-quarter earnings, surpassing expectations. The company disclosed earnings of $1.07 per share, beating the consensus estimate of $1.02 per share. Quarterly sales stood at $1.171 billion, exceeding the estimated $1.134 billion. For FY2026, TransUnion anticipates adjusted EPS between $4.63 and $4.71, lower than the market's $4.82 estimate. Sales for the year are projected to range from $4.946 billion to $4.981 billion, compared to estimates of $4.927 billion. President and CEO Chris Cartwright expressed satisfaction with the performance, highlighting a revenue growth of 13%, primarily driven by the solid performance in U.S. Markets, especially Financial Services.

data.title: Lincoln Electric Holdings (NASDAQ: LECO) beats Q4 EPS estimate at $2.65 per share but misses on sales at $1.079B. CEO optimistic about strong results.
data.description: Lincoln Electric Holdings (NASDAQ: LECO) released its fourth-quarter results, revealing earnings of $2.65 per share, surpassing the expected $2.54 per share predicted by analysts. However, the company's quarterly sales were reported at $1.079 billion, falling short of the anticipated $1.099 billion. Chairman and CEO Steven B. Hedlund expressed satisfaction with the outcome, highlighting record sales, adjusted EPS, and cash returns to shareholders in 2025. He also emphasized the company's successful navigation of the current operational landscape by leveraging growth prospects, managing expenses, and restructuring the business to enhance its market dominance.

data.title: Advance Auto Parts stock remains flat after strong Q4, beating EPS estimate of 42 cents with 86 cents. Quarterly sales reach $1.973 billion.
data.description: Advance Auto Parts Inc. (NYSE: AAP) experienced a minimal change in stock value on Friday following a successful fourth quarter report. The company surpassed expectations with an adjusted earnings per share of 86 cents, exceeding the analyst projection of 42 cents. Their quarterly sales reached $1.973 billion, surpassing the anticipated $1.952 billion by the Street. The inclusion of an additional week in the fourth quarter of 2025 contributed around $132 million to net sales. Additionally, the fourth-quarter comparable sales growth was noted at 1.1%, with sales demonstrating a positive trend over the final eight weeks of the period. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Corsair Gaming stock soars as they beat Q4 expectations with revenue at $436.9 million, +6% YoY, and adjusted EBITDA up 84%.
data.description: Gaming hardware maker Corsair Gaming, Inc. (NASDAQ: CRSR) saw a surge in shares on Friday following a strong fourth-quarter performance. The company reported adjusted EPS of 43 cents, surpassing the 27 cents consensus estimate. Revenue for the quarter reached $436.9 million, a 6% increase year over year, beating expectations of $421.99 million. For the full year 2025, Corsair posted a revenue growth of 12% to $1.47 billion, with GAAP gross profit rising by 30% to $425.9 million. Adjusted EBITDA also saw a significant increase of approximately 84%. The Gaming Components and Systems segment led the performance with a 16% revenue increase driven by high demand for memory products. Despite semiconductor constraints, Corsair demonstrated its ability to secure supply, solidifying its position in the performance memory market. In addition, the company announced its inaugural share buyback program.

data.title: Crocs Inc (NASDAQ: CROX) reported Q4 earnings of $2.29/share, exceeding estimates. Sales totaled $957.640 million. FY2026 EPS guidance $12.88-$13.35. Strategic actions to enhance brand health.
data.description: Footwear company Crocs Inc (NASDAQ: CROX) has announced impressive fourth-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations. They reported earnings of $2.29 per share, beating the expected $1.90 per share. The company also exceeded projected sales, reaching $957.640 million compared to an estimated $922.327 million. Crocs has provided a positive outlook for the first quarter, with adjusted EPS guidance above estimates. For the full fiscal year of 2026, they foresee adjusted EPS ranging from $12.88 to $13.35, surpassing market estimates of $12.10. Crocs experienced robust performance in 2025, achieving revenue exceeding $4 billion, driven by strong international growth in the Crocs Brand. The company remains focused on strategic initiatives to enhance long-term sustainability. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: The Victory Bancorp, Inc. reported Q4 2025 financial results: $396.3M net loans, $412.2M deposits, $3.82M net interest income, $448K net income.
data.description: The Victory Bancorp, Inc. announced strong financial results for Q4 2025 with notable highlights including a $396.3 million increase in net loans, $412.2 million in total deposits, $3.82 million in net interest income, and $448 thousand in net income for the quarter. Stockholders' equity rose to $32.2 million, and the company ended the year without any outstanding borrowings. Asset quality metrics were impressive, with nonperforming assets at 0.00% and net charge-offs at 0.01%. The bank leader, Joseph W. Major, emphasized the balance sheet strength and dedication to client service. Victory Bancorp, Inc. trades under the symbol VTYB and is the parent company of The Victory Bank, offering a full range of banking services to the local community.

data.title: Dow Jones fluctuates after unexpected CPI report, DraftKings drops on earnings. Applied Materials sees rise.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index showed uncertainty following a CPI inflation report that was lower than anticipated. DraftKings experienced a significant drop in its stock value due to earnings. Additionally, Applied Materials saw a notable increase in its stock price. This dynamic movement in the stock market was influenced by the surprising inflation data. The fluctuations in the market were closely followed throughout the day, with live coverage providing updates on these developments.
